qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Fix: Push-analyst grant TTL too short (180s) causes expired grants. agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7 sets PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180. After dispatching push-analyst and waiting for it to complete, the orchestrator then processes the result and calls execute-push.py. This round-trip frequently exceeds 180 seconds, causing the grant to be expired when execute-push.py reads it. Increase PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS to 600 (10 minutes).

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: content grep in agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7 Python snippet

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS changed from 180 to 600 at agents/push-analyst.md:166; test assertions updated to assert 600 at test_final_sweep.sh lines 64/73/86; commands/push.md TTL section updated to document 600s and removed stale 180s prose

ac_results.AC1: pass — grep 'PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 600' on agents/push-analyst.md returns exactly 1 match at line 166; grep 'PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180' returns 0 matches in assignment context (only historical mention at line 161 is acceptable per spec)
ac_results.AC2: pass — hunk-level diff of all 3 target files confirmed: (a) agents/push-analyst.md diff shows only TTL constant line and 4-line adjacent comment changed (lines 159-162 + 166); (b) commands/push.md diff shows only the Push-analyst grant TTL section changed (lines 216-226); (c) hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh diff shows only lines 64/73/86 changed. File-list proof caveat: commands/push.md and hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh were already dirty at task start (present in baseline_dirty_snapshot), so raw 'git diff --name-only d06a656' subtracts only agents/push-analyst.md as newly dirty. AC2 is verified via hunk/content inspection, not file-list subtraction alone. Untracked files in working tree are workflow artifacts (docs/dev/ JSONs, specs/) not touched by T3.
ac_results.AC3: pass — hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh verified: line 64 grep pattern uses 600 ('^PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*600[[:space:]]*$'); line 73 asserts 'val.value == 600'; line 86 pattern is '600[[:space:]]*(s|seconds?)|TTL.*600|PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS.*600'. No 180 values found on any of lines 64/73/86. Note on line 86: pattern can also match historical 'raised from 180s to 600s' mention in push.md, but AC4 provides the definitive proof that current TTL assignment is 600 and stale 180 prose is absent.
ac_results.AC4: pass — commands/push.md contains 'PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 600' at line 219; 'The 600s TTL is the named constant' at line 223; '600s' appears at lines 219/220/223; stale phrases 'PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180' (as current-value assignment) and 'The 180s TTL is the named constant' are absent (grep returns 0 matches). Historical 'raised from 180s to 600s' mention at lines 220/223 is acceptable per spec.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 5 findings: (1) AC2 file-list proof is confounded by pre-existing dirty files — commands/push.md and test_final_sweep.sh were already in baseline_dirty_snapshot; only agents/push-analyst.md is newly dirty; (2) untracked files omitted from scope claim; (3) test harness execution not reported; (4) line-86 regex can match historical 180->600 mention, not just current assignment — not a blocker; (5) verdict should note AC2 caveat.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied findings (1)/(2)/(3)/(5): AC2 evidence section rewritten to document hunk/content verification as the primary proof rather than file-list subtraction; untracked files explicitly classified as workflow artifacts; bash hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh was run and T3-scope AC4 V1/V2/V3/V4 PASS confirmed; AC3 line-86 note clarified that AC4 provides definitive proof. Finding (4) noted as not a blocker. Verdict remains PASS with the AC2 caveat documented.
